content.problemStatement:
Chef has a tasty ingredient ― an integer KKK. He defines a tasty matrix AAA with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM columns (numbered 111 through MMM) as Ai,j=K+i+jA_{i, j} = K + i + jAi,j​=K+i+j for each valid i,ji,ji,j.

Currently, Chef is busy in the kitchen making this tasty matrix. Help him find the bitwise XOR of all elements of this matrix.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2000000
The sum of M over all test cases does not exceed 2000000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers N, M and K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the bitwise XOR of all elements A[i][j] = K + i + j of the N×M matrix.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first case the matrix is [[7,8],[8,9]] and 7⊕8⊕8⊕9 = 14. For the second case the matrix is [[9,10,11],[10,11,12]] and its XOR is 5.
Matrix has a single element K+1+1 = 3, so the XOR is 3.
Rows produce XORs 0, 28, 0 respectively; total XOR = 28.

solution.approach: For a fixed row i (1‑based) the elements are K+i+1, K+i+2, … , K+i+M. This is a consecutive range of length M. The XOR of a range [L,R] can be obtained in O(1) using the well‑known function f(x) = XOR of all numbers from 0 to x, which depends only on x mod 4. For each row we compute rowXor = f(K+i+M) ^ f(K+i), and XOR all rowXor values. Because the total sum of N over all test cases is ≤2·10⁶, iterating over rows is fast enough. The algorithm runs in O(total N) time and O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total N) across all test cases (≤ 2·10⁶)
